chapter_goal: Classify toric manifolds by Delzant polytopes and make the construction sketch executable.

source_span_read.title: Classification of Symplectic Toric Manifolds
source_span_read.source_note: Read the local PDF section on Delzant polytopes, primitive normals, Delzant theorem, the construction sketch from facets and exact sequences, and the interval example reducing to CP^1.

library_routing.representation: polygon panels with primitive normals and determinant checks
library_routing.why: simplicity, rationality, and smoothness are visible at facets and vertices

library_routing.representation: standard simplex with inequalities and outward normals
library_routing.why: the construction uses normals and lambda constants, not only vertices
classification and construction are a chain of data transformations
pi: R^d -> R^n and its kernel are algebraic gates in the construction

library_routing.representation: zero-level radius and diagonal S^1 quotient
library_routing.why: the simplest Delzant construction is concrete enough for numerical checks

visual_sequence.inspection_target: read which vertices pass primitive determinant tests and which fail smoothness
visual_sequence.validation: standard simplex determinant defects vanish; bad triangle defect is nonzero

visual_sequence.concept: Half-space description by primitive outward normals
visual_sequence.inspection_target: match each facet inequality with its primitive outward normal
visual_sequence.validation: all normals are primitive and active pairs define vertices

visual_sequence.inspection_target: trace toric data to moment image and Delzant polytope to reduction
visual_sequence.validation: route graph has the expected path from polytope to toric manifold

visual_sequence.inspection_target: see how d facet normals produce pi, N, and the dual projection
connect Delta=[0,a], diagonal subgroup, zero sphere, and projective quotient
